Free Guide · One-Time Download

The 12 Watch Hill spots that don't show up on the tourist maps.

A free PDF map of the places locals actually go — quiet beaches, a hidden trail that ends at the sound, the lighthouse view nobody photographs, the bakery that closes when it's sold out. Sent immediately when you subscribe.

One email a month after that — usually a "what's worth doing this month" note. Unsubscribe anytime, link in every email.

What's in it

Twelve spots. Real ones. Local ones.

No top-10 lists pulled from somebody else's blog. No "must-see" places where the actual must-see is the parking lot. These are the spots I send my own friends to when they ask.

  • 01

    A two-page hand-illustrated map

    Roughly south-to-north along the coast, so you can work it into one day or stretch it across a long weekend.

  • 02

    Best-time-to-go notes for each spot

    Most places have one window when they're magic and one window when they're miserable. I tell you which is which.

  • 03

    The three I'd start with

    If you only have one afternoon, I'll tell you exactly which three to do, in what order, and why.

  • 04

    One spot only locals know

    I'm not joking. This one isn't on Google. It's on the map.

Who it's for

People planning a trip — or people who already love it here.

First-time visitors

You've heard "Watch Hill is great" and now you're trying to figure out what that actually means. Start here.

Returning regulars

You come every summer and you've done the carousel. You're ready for the next layer.

Day trippers from CT / MA / NY

You've got eight hours. I'll help you make them count.

Who's writing this

I live here. That's the whole pitch.

Watch Hill Guide is written from Westerly, Rhode Island — not from a content farm, not from a syndicated network, not by someone who came once on assignment. Every place on the map is somewhere I've been more times than I can count, in different seasons, in different weather, at different hours.

The map is free because it's also an introduction. If we get along over email, eventually I might write a longer paid guide. If we don't, you keep the map and unsubscribe — no hard feelings.

Ready for the map?

Free, immediate, no upsell. Just the PDF and an honest welcome.

Subscribe and the map is on its way in under a minute.
